title: poa nemoralis subsp. hypanica
intro: Poa nemoralis subsp. hypanica, also known as wood meadow-grass or wood sedge-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the genus Poa and is native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource for herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
